lineage: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.

Eunomius separates from Eudoxius; a Disturbance is raised at Alexandria by Eudoxius, and Athanasius flees into Voluntary Exile again, but in Consequence of the Clamors of the People the Emperor recalls and re-establishes him in his See.

ccel.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

title: Eunomius separates from Eudoxius; a Disturbance is raised at Alexandria by Eudoxius, and Athanasius flees into Voluntary Exile again, but in Consequence of the Clamors of the People the Emperor recalls and re-establishes him in his See.

source.title: NPNF2-02. Socrates and Sozomenus Ecclesiastical Histories

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: About the same time Eunomius separated himself from Eudoxius, and held assemblies apart, because after he had repeatedly entreated that his preceptor Aëtius might be received into communion, Eudoxius continued to oppose it.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now Eudoxius did this against his preference, for he did not reject the opinion with Aëtius since it was the same as his own; but he yielded to the prevailing sentiment of his own party, who objected to Aëtius as heterodox.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: This was the cause of the division between Eunomius and Eudoxius, and such was the state of things at Constantinople.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: But the church at Alexandria was disturbed by an edict of the prætorian prefects, sent hither by means of Eudoxius.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Whereupon Athanasius, dreading the irrational impetuosity of the multitude, and fearing lest he should be regarded as the author of the excesses that might be committed, concealed himself for four entire months in an ancestral tomb.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Inasmuch however as the people, on account of their affection for him, became seditious in impatience of his absence, the emperor, on ascertaining that on this account agitation prevailed at Alexandria, ordered by his letters that Athanasius should be suffered to preside over the churches without molestation; and this was the reason why the Alexandrian church enjoyed tranquillity until the death of Athanasius.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: How the Arian faction became possessed of the churches after his decease, we shall unfold in the course of our history.
